name: neural-weights-en-gb
$staged_candidate_version_comment: Lockstep with `neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json`'s own `staged_candidate_version` (final-review fix, 2026-07-23) — version numbers are assigned ON PROMOTE, not on staging. The base checkpoint this overlay depends on stayed a staged, un-promoted candidate (see `phase` below) and the `v3.11.1-deploc-consolidate` repair attempt came back NOT CLEAN (stop rule executed, lineage closed) — so this overlay's OWN `version` also stays at 6.6.0, not 6.7.0.
$comment_new_card: NEW in 6.7.0 (Task 8, placetype-pair-prior arc, 2026-07-23). The overlay previously shipped NO model-card.json of its own — `resolveFromPackageDir` fell back to `neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json` (the #1177 card-less-overlay path, `source` suffixed `+base`) so the classifier had a label vocab to decode against. This card is now overlay-LOCAL and, per the #1249 base-card-fallback design, resolveFromPackageDir prefers it over the base card — the fallback becomes dormant for en-gb from this version forward (still live for any future overlay that ships none). `labels`/`requires`/`architecture`/`components_supported` below are duplicated VERBATIM from the base card so decode behavior is byte-identical to the pre-6.7.0 fallback; only the provenance/eval/notes content is GB-specific. See `neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json` (same version) for the full training/provenance record this card doesn't repeat.
model_lineage: Data-only overlay. Ships NO model.onnx/tokenizer.model of its own — resolves both from `@mailwoman/neural-weights-en-us` via `mailwoman.baseWeights` (byte-identical artifact; one encoder serves both locales). What this package ships: `postcode-gb.bin` (GB outward-code postcode-anchor binary, built from the WOF GB postcode shard) and `pair-index-gb.bin` (the placetype-pair-prior arc's PIX1 retrieval index, built from HM Land Registry PPD tuples). See `neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json` (its `staged_candidate_files_md5`/`eval` blocks, `staged_candidate_version` 6.7.0) for the base model's full training provenance (v3.11.0-deploc-feed step-8000, "feed-8k").
phase: STAGED-CANDIDATE-CLOSED — mirrors the base card's status exactly (same encoder, same ship blocker). The base's Gauntlet metamorphic-layer FAIL (Task 8, 2026-07-23) was NOT resolved by a same-day repair attempt (`v3.11.1-deploc-consolidate`) — that run came back NOT CLEAN (comma-drop break byte-identical 8k→10k) and the STOP RULE was executed, closing the v3.11.x lineage for shipping. This overlay is blocked the same way, since en-gb has no encoder of its own to grade separately. GB-specific artifacts (postcode-gb.bin, pair-index-gb.bin) are independently graded below and clear every registered bar on their own — the block is entirely inherited from the base checkpoint decision, not a GB-specific finding. Path forward: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-v312-comma-robust-recipe.md` (v3.12, operator-gated redesign). This overlay's own code (postcode-gb.bin build, pair-index-gb.bin build + release-path wiring) ships regardless, composing with the currently-shipped v385 (6.6.0) base — see `@mailwoman/neural-weights-en-us`'s `model-card.json` `phase` field for the full base-model narrative.

base_model.resolution: mailwoman.baseWeights in package.json — resolveWeights/resolveFromPackageDir resolve model.onnx + tokenizer.model from the base package dir (source suffixed "+base"); this card's own labels/requires/architecture below are a verbatim copy of the base's, not re-derived at runtime — keep them in lockstep on every base ship (same discipline as neural-weights-en-gb/scripts/link-dev-weights.ts's DEFAULT_MODEL/DEFAULT_TOKENIZER md5 lockstep comment).

postcode_gb_bin.granularity: outward code only (e.g. "SW1A", not the full unit "SW1A 1AA") — 2.7M unit postcodes is both too large for the browser budget and finer-grained than an anchor needs; a full unit code that misses the exact lookup falls back to its outward centroid automatically (extractPostcodeAnchors)
postcode_gb_bin.not_committed: derived artifact, .gitignore'd, materialized by scripts/copy-weights.ts at release time and scripts/link-dev-weights.ts for local dev — same posture as postcode-fr.bin

pair_index_gb_bin.format: PIX1 placetype-pair index (placetype-pair-prior arc, Task 3) — a retrieval-augmented (child, parent) dependent_locality prior, e.g. ("Fishburn", "Stockton-on-Tees")
pair_index_gb_bin.source: HM Land Registry Price Paid Data (PPD) tuples CSV, $MAILWOMAN_DATA_ROOT/ppd/2026-07-22/gb-tuples.csv, md5 dbd675bdc970ea60f96f0a470429a128, 25,674,048 rows
pair_index_gb_bin.delta_calibration: δ=5.0 (Task 7, .superpowers/sdd/task-7-report.md, 2026-07-22) — SUPERSEDES Task 3's δ=6.0 rung-3 probe placeholder. Calibrated via a δ ∈ {3,4,4.5,5,6,7} sweep against a held-out PPD-tail set (dependent_locality tag-correct recall, n=653) and the 6,500-row FSA venue-confound falsifier board (FP), separately for both v3.11.0-deploc-feed ship-candidate checkpoints — they calibrate to DIFFERENT optima (feed-2k → 4.5, feed-8k → 5.0). This artifact bakes feed-8k's value (5.0), matching Task 7's checkpoint recommendation (see the base card's phase/model_lineage). δ is a genuine recall/FP dial, NOT a free lunch — FP grows monotonically with δ (roughly 6-10× from δ=3 to δ=7 on both checkpoints), so the smallest δ clearing the recall bar is load-bearing, not a formality.
pair_index_gb_bin.probe_mode: "segment" (whole comma-delimited segments only) — the library default since d2a1242f. The retired "window" (sliding sub-word) mode measured 52.1% venue-confound FP against a pre-registered FP=0 bar and is opt-in-only now; segment mode cuts that to <1% at the calibrated δ, at the cost of going fully inert on comma-stripped input (see eval.gb_golden_comma_stripped below — a documented v1 trade, not a bug).
pair_index_gb_bin.country_gate: hard-gated — the prior only fires when the resolved locale's country matches the index header's country (gb). Never crosses into en-us parses (see the base card's en-us companion test / neural/test/weights.test.ts).
pair_index_gb_bin.not_committed: derived artifact, .gitignore'd (same posture as postcode-gb.bin) — rebuilt via `mailwoman gazetteer pair-index --out neural-weights-en-gb --country gb --source <ppd-csv> --delta 5.0`

architecture.$comment: Verbatim copy of the base card's architecture — same encoder, no GB-specific change.

requires.$comment: Verbatim copy of the base card's requires block — same trained ship-config, same fail-closed contract via createScorer. Keep in lockstep with neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json on every base ship.

notes: First graded GB story (Task 8, 2026-07-23) — before this, the overlay was packaging scaffolding only (README's own prior wording: 'GB-specific per-tag evaluation numbers are not yet published'). GB dependent_locality full-pipeline recall (base checkpoint + pair-index prior ON @ δ=5.0): 69/69 emission, 67/69 tag-correct (97.1%), on the 69-row gb-golden dependent_locality board. The three-way ablation (base card's eval.gate_2026_07_23_v3110_deploc_feed_8k.dependent_locality_ablation) shows this recall is carried almost entirely by the PRIOR, not the checkpoint's own resurrected classifier row: v385 (pre-feed) + prior @ δ=6 alone already gets 3/69; the feed checkpoint with the prior OFF gets 0/69; only prior ON @ the calibrated δ reaches 67/69. Comma-stripped input is fully inert (0/69) — the documented segment-mode v1 trade, not a regression. Venue-confound FP at δ=5.0: 48/6,500 (0.738%), well under the retired δ=6.0 working number (122/6,500, 1.877%). The two GB misses (both already characterized in Task 6): 'Goulbourne Road, St Georges, Telford, TF2 9LE' (word-boundary, wrong-value) and '101 Coniston Avenue, Knott End on Sea, Poulton-le-Fylde' (pre-existing model-level miss, independent of the prior).

gb_golden_comma_stripped.note: fully inert — the documented segment-probe-mode v1 trade (probe mode only matches whole comma-delimited segments), not a bug

delta_sweep_feed_8k.$comment: held-out dependent_locality tag-correct recall (n=653) / venue-confound FP (n=6500), both checkpoints swept — feed-8k row only (this card's checkpoint); see the base card / task-7-report.md for feed-2k's row.

eval.gauntlet: not separately run for this overlay — see the base card's eval.gate_2026_07_23_v3110_deploc_feed_8k.gauntlet. en-gb ships no encoder of its own to grade; the base checkpoint's metamorphic-layer FAIL applies here by inheritance.

format.$comment: Model/tokenizer format is the base's — see neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json. This overlay's own artifacts:
